reviews.review_description:
Really good game to play. Lots of fun but isn't popular for some reason.
The game has a really fun twist on Poker for the virtual world that I love, as it keeps the game interesting. But i do wish the gameplay was more fluid. For example, there is a counter you have to turn over to let people know you are ready to play. I feel this should only happen at the beginning of a game, and if someone hasn't done this by a certain amount of time, they get kicked out to prevent game stagnation. 
But there is very rarely anyone other than myself playing the game, which is really frustrating as it's so fun to play in comparison to other poker games i have on meta that get quite boring after a while. I usually only ever see one other person playing at any one time of being on it, if im lucky. 
I've tried sharing it with "friends" on Meta, but it's not shared in an effective way. 
It needs more promoting

reviews.review_title: Cool vibes, but I had trouble learning
reviews.review_description:
I checked out Death Game Hotel as a total newbie, knowing nothing about poker or how it's played.

I'll start with my praise: the environments are really cool! The lobby has a cool lounge vibe and even a playable piano, and there are a handful of really fun, thematic rooms to play poker in. One is gothic, another is very asian in decore, there's one in a microgravity space station (yeah, you *can* toss floating objects at your opponents), etc. 

On top of the cool environments, the game also gives you objects to taunt your opponents with, such as the laser gun. There are VR-ified cheating mechanics, and you can gamble away your body parts. There are slots, a gacha machine, and two types of card games. If regular poker is water, Death Game Hotel is a fun, spicy tea that is best enjoy with friends.

The major problem for me is that I found the tutorial to be an insufficient teaching tool as someone who has never played poker ever before. I just got overwhelmed. The game *does* have a tutorial that explains the rules, but what I need is a gameified tutorial - a guided and tutorialized match against a computer. I ended up quiting because I couldn't get a solid grasp on the basics of poker, let alone Death Game Hotel's playful additions such a cheating, gambling body parts, etc. I did playtest this game, and provided that feedback, so hopefully they make this change and the game becomes more accessible to newbies like me! I did talk to some testers who were able to get ahold of how to play (despite their having never played poker before), so your mileage may vary.

The great thing is, it's free, so why not give it a shot and see.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for such a thoughtful and detailed review.
We truly appreciate hearing from someone approaching the game as a complete poker newcomer.

We’re glad you enjoyed the environments, themed rooms, stash items, and the body-part wagering mechanic. The atmosphere and social experience are core parts of what we aim to deliver.

At the same time, your feedback about the tutorial feeling overwhelming is very important.
While the game explains the rules, we understand that a more gameified, guided tutorial — such as a structured practice match against AI — could make a big difference for brand-new players. This is something we are actively considering as we continue improving accessibility.

Death Game Hotel is an evolving title, and making the experience more welcoming to newcomers is a priority for us.

If you’re open to it, we’d love to hear more on our official Discord. It’s also a place to find matches, share suggestions, and communicate directly with the team.

Thank you again for your honest feedback.

reviews.review_description:
Death Game Hotel is a refreshing and twisted take on gambling-style games, standing far apart from anything traditional. Instead of sticking to one formula, the game offers several unique modes—Death Poker, Slots, Gotcha, and the Goblet Game—each with its own rules and surprises. Tutorials are available for every game, making it easy to jump in without feeling lost.

You can play solo or multiplayer, but trust me—multiplayer is where this game truly shines. The chaos, laughter, and tension are amplified when playing with others, especially when you start buying interactive toys that everyone can use during matches.

The Goblet Game is a standout. The goal is simple: get rid of all your tiles first—but the ever-present threat of the Reaper keeps every round tense. What really raises the stakes is the game’s brutal wagering system. You’re not just betting chips—you’re betting body parts. Lose your hand and you can’t hold cards. Lose your mouth and your voice becomes robotic, making communication difficult. Lose your eyes and your vision becomes bloody and distorted. Lose your ears and sound turns mechanical and disorienting. These twists create total chaos in the best way possible. Thankfully, you can recover by eating food—if you survive long enough.

Death Poker builds on classic poker mechanics but adds a deadly new suit that forces tougher decisions and unexpected outcomes. One of the most entertaining mechanics is the ability to cheat—as long as you don’t get caught. It adds another layer of tension and strategy that keeps every round unpredictable.

On top of the gameplay, customization is surprisingly deep. You can personalize your avatar with unique skins, earn badges, and use expressive stamps to communicate, which fits perfectly with the game’s dark yet playful tone.

Overall, Death Game Hotel is chaotic, clever, and wildly fun—especially with friends. If you’re looking for a multiplayer experience that blends strategy, deception, and dark humor, this is it!

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for taking the time to write such a thoughtful and in-depth review.
Seeing the game’s systems, themes, and mechanics reflected back in such detail truly means a lot to the entire development team.

We’re especially happy that you highlighted Goblet of the Reaper, including the constant presence of the Reaper and the tension created by wagering body parts.
The fact that losing body parts doesn’t just change visuals, but directly affects gameplay and communication, is a core part of what we wanted to achieve.

Your breakdown of Death Poker, including the new suit and the risk–reward balance around cheating, perfectly captures our intent: a constantly shifting mind game where every decision can come at a cost.

We also appreciate you mentioning customization and expressive tools like stamps. Balancing a dark, brutal theme with playful social expression was important to us, and it’s great to hear that it resonated.

We’ll continue expanding and refining the experience through updates and events.
For news, finding other players, or sharing feedback and bug reports directly with the team, we’d love to see you on our official Discord.

reviews.review_description:
I enjoyed this game a lot. I really liked both the goblet-filling strategy and the twisted take on poker. I’m not usually into poker games, but this version was actually enjoyable. The game does a great job helping you understand what kind of hand you have, and the cheatsheet was super helpful for wrapping my head around all the different hand combinations.

The ghost card is a cool addition and adds something extra that keeps the gameplay interesting.

It’s a great game to play with friends, but I also really enjoyed the solo mode. One thing I do wish they’d add is more avatars and clothing options—I’d love to customize my character.

It would be nice if the multiplayer lobbies had password protection so you could easily play just with your friends instead of needing it for the poker game itself. It was also a little confusing figuring out how to actually enter a poker game. I like that the slot area has a big, obvious door, so something similar for poker—like an elevator to a poker room or a clearly marked door—would help a lot. Even if the game still launches from the wrist device, having a physical spot in the hotel lobby would make it much easier to find, especially since the other two game areas are called out so boldly.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your detailed and thoughtful review.
We’re really glad to hear that you enjoyed Goblet of the Reaper and Death Poker, especially if poker isn’t usually your go-to genre.

We also appreciate your comments on the hand indicators and cheatsheet.
Those were designed to help players understand the game as they play, so it’s great to know they were useful.

The “ghost card” you mentioned is what we call the Death Suit in-game.
It’s meant to introduce unexpected decisions and keep each round feeling tense and unpredictable.

Regarding multiplayer lobbies, password-protected lobbies are already available.
Next time you play, we encourage you to try using a lobby password to easily enjoy matches with just your friends.

Your feedback about finding and entering poker games more intuitively is also very helpful, and we’ll keep it in mind as we continue improving the overall flow of the hotel.

We’ll continue expanding the experience through updates to make it even more enjoyable for everyone.

reviews.review_description: This game is a delightfully messy party experience that puts a clever twist on classic casino games, especially poker. It feels familiar if you’ve played things like PokerStars VR or Balatro, but it adds its own personality with wild rules, high risk wagers, and chaotic fun where you can gamble more than just chips. Beyond poker, there are slots and a Goblet style game that ends up being one of the most fun activities overall. The environments are lively and varied, with the space themed one really standing out, and the art style and avatars are unique and full of character. The lobby itself is packed with interactive distractions like food, drinks, and even a piano, which makes hanging out between games just as fun as playing. The inclusive communication options are a great touch and make the game accessible even without voice chat. The biggest downside is the player count, since the game truly shines with friends and can feel a bit empty when lobbies are quiet. Overall, it is a stylish, chaotic, and memorable multiplayer experience that works best as a shared party game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review.
We’re glad you enjoyed the chaotic party experience that comes from our twist on classic casino games like Death Poker, especially when played with friends. We’re also happy you highlighted the lobby activities — from props to the piano — as part of the overall fun between matches.

We appreciate your kind words about the non-voice communication options, such as preset messages and stamps. Making the game accessible and enjoyable in different play styles is something we care deeply about.

We also understand your feedback regarding player population. Helping players connect more easily and making group play shine is an ongoing focus for us, and we’ll continue improving the experience through updates.

reviews.review_description:
I was hesitant, but I actually really enjoyed my time. When you first join, it's a little confusing. There are some tutorials, but you have to find them in the menu and take the time to watch them. Let's be honest, who watches tutorials anyway? You'll have a better first experience if you find someone in a lobby that is willing to give you a tour.

The goblet game is pretty fun, albeit, also confusing at first. Watch the tutorial or talk to a seasoned player. Once you understand, you'll enjoy it!

Death poker is a fun twist on Texas Hold em. Not only are there death cards, you also can cheat, sort of. If you know poker, there is a hand ranking tablet that will show you how to play with the death cards.

The slot machines are exactly what you'd expect. I don't enjoy slots, so I didn't care for them in this game. If you like slots, you'll love it.

Overall, it's a very neat social game. Meet new friends or bring some with you. You get costumes and have props you can play with. Play some games and risk losing body parts, which is a pretty entertaining experience. Can't use your left hand to pick up cards, can't see well, can't hear well, can't talk. It's a fun twist.  If any of this sounded interesting, give it a go!

reviews.review_description:
Note that I am here because I was testing the multiplayer, and I haven't yet tried the single player.  I'll update the review when I do.

This game has its issues that definitely need fixing, but it's still an absolute blast to play every time.  You have two primary game modes (one with a 2-player variant) that both have their own interesting twists.

The first one is Poker, but it has a couple twists.  The big twist is a 5th "death" suit, which will be either beat all normal hands if you can use the death card(s), or will be the worst possible hand if you can't match the death card.  An interesting counterbalance to that is that you can cheat so you're able to sneak in a death card and maybe win, or sneak one out of your hand to avoid losing.

The other game mode is Goblet which is an interesting shedding game.  If you've never played a shedding card game, the goal is to play all your cards before anyone else.  With Goblet, these are tiles you place into goblets and it's a really interesting guessing/deducing/bluffing game trying to get rid of tiles with special powers in the right way.  Like Poker it can also be helped by cheating.

Both game modes involve betting body parts and you can heal by eating food you gamble for in gachapon machines.  There are also a variety of toys to play with such as a roulette gun and a cute swimming turtle.  For the price of free...just go check it out.  I love it and look forward to seeing them update it to be better.

reviews.review_description:
It’s free, so it is certainly worth checking out. It is an interesting and gory twist on gambling. With a Texas Hold-Em style poker game, where a single card can either make your hand the worst hand or better than a normal straight flush. All while wagering your own limbs in some cases. Throw in your left hand and lose, now watch it get chopped off and you can’t hold anything. Lose your voice and sound strange to others, or your ears and everyone else sounds strange to you. Same goes for the other games, with the goblets that you try to avoid running over, but try and cheat out without getting caught. Maybe you can try the slot machines and get “lucky” when your eyes get gauged out for a few extra 100 coins. Maybe you just want to play a standard gatcha machine and spend your coins on consumables that can be used as buffs in games or help restore your lost limbs. If you don’t need them right away, you can always go back to the lobby and feast on the infinite coffee and donuts. 
The game is interesting, but, you are relying on others to join your lobby unless you pay for the single player DLC. With that you get AI opponents and the ability to acquire their skins after defeating them. The bad part about jumping into multiplayer games is that you might not have any clue what is going on and without some beginners luck, there is a good chance of losing. There isn’t a practice tutorial, but it does give you directions and a help tablet that you can keep on display in game. It may take a while to get to understand it, but once you do, it’s pretty funny watching others get dismembered. 
Being that it is free, it is sure to attract kids, but this isn’t a game for kids. The dark theme, gambling, drinking and smoking might not be the things you want your kids to be virtualizing. 
The not understanding the games off hand, required to wait for others and the expiring currency that you have to use or lose are easy to overlook once you realize it all. Regardless, it is an interes

reviews.review_description:
This game takes the fun of gambling and cranks it up to a whole new deadly level. It’s a mini casino in VR with slot machines, an interesting goblet game, and the wildest version of Texas Hold ’Em you’ve ever played. In addition to betting chips, you can also risk betting your eyes, ears, or even your hands. Losing a hand could mean actually losing your hand and being left with a bloody stump.

Poker is fast, fun, and full of bluffing, and the added death cards give every hand a chance to flip the entire table. There’s also an interesting cheat system that can help you win. If you’re low on chips, you can always try your luck at the slot machines to win some quick money. The goblet game is also really unique, though it takes a few minutes to learn.

Because it’s free, you and all your friends can play together. The only real downside is that once you run out of chips, you either have to buy more or wait until the next day for the daily bonus.

reviews.review_title: Mandatory organ donation when house wins
reviews.review_description: This game is surprisingly addictive.  It is set in a casino atmosphere, but something is just a bit... off.  The designers really nailed the dark and twisty humor genre that I tend to enjoy.  The details shine through with the stormy weather seen through tall glass windows, random blood splatter and pooling on most surfaces, and even the shady (avatar) characters we get to play.   My Repo: The Genetic Opera-lovin' heart felt right at home upon spawn-in.  They were even thoughtful enough to consider the gore-factor... so I played with gore disabled at first, which meant that my user saw a less-detailed, more-cartoonish version when the reaper came to collect.  I recommend bringing friends to play, because the public lobbies are hit or miss.  Plus, what's more fun than seeing your friends bet their eyes or mouth when you feel you've got the winning hand?!  Once you are all gathered in your private lobby (password protected), this game becomes a multi-room experience.  We can hang out in the lobby, play piano, sit at the bar, enjoy the free concessions (donuts, coffee - which are restorative if you've lost an organ), or we can head over to the slots room.  If we're up for some competition, we can create a private game from our menu (or the lobby kiosks); there are 2 options for a novice to start: a strategic tile game with goblets for 3-6 players -or- poker, which requires 2-6 players.  After playing a few sessions, you can unlock the 3rd game which is a PvP version of the goblet game.  Each game is multi-rounded, so you have a chance to redeem yourself if you start out poorly.  But in the end, the reaper comes to collect his winnings.  Chips are bet during gameplay, but final bets always include either eyes, ears, mouth, left hand, or right hand... and no one is exempt.  The diabolical part is that YOU get to choose which part you'll lose.  This game was definitely a surprise, and I find myself wanting to play again and again.

reviews.review_description:
The review is on the free2play multiplayer component only. 

A very well thought out collection of games that both have a significant amount of nuisance and tactic involved.

The multiplayer is split up mainly into two game types the Goblet Game and Death Poker.

Death Poker is poker with a twist, where there are cards can be dealt as a special death card.
Any hand that is played incorporating a death card is basically stronger than a full house.

So a simple pair of 3 (with one 3 being a death 3) beats a full house of regular cards

The Goblet game is harder to explain but is a game of tactics.
The main aim is to get rid off all your tiles
There are multiple cups on the table which have an unknown amount of room in them.
You take turns trying not to over fill the cups.
The tactics come in form of special tiles that have unique effects.
It may be as simple as forcing all players to draw a tile, or skipping a players turn.
But there are trap tile that will cause a goblet to over fill when someone play a tile in there next.
There are an lot of tiles to learn and take advantage off.

What's interesting about both multiplayer games is that you can cheat.
Switch cards in your poker hand or slot a tile in a cup for at no risk of over flowing

For players that have a group of friend they can convince to download the game and play with them (as there are no bots in multiplayer)
The game is an easy 5/5 free multiplayer.
For others relying on a public lobby, you may have mixed results in finding a match depending on the time of day you hop on.

Im hoping my review will help the game gain a few more players and it truly deserves it as one of the better free multiplayer games on the store.

Im hoping the developers continue and expand on shop items to have an alternative competitor to Poker Stars (with some extra layers of tactics and deceit)

reviews.review_title: A Interesting Take on Poker and Slots
reviews.review_description:
Do you like gambling? what am i saying of course you do, and this game allows you to raise the stakes like never before. I played the poker and slots, and not only is this an amazing poker sim, but with the mechanic where you can bet your body parts and the new suite type, it makes for a dynamic twist on the standard formula of poker. That being said, there are three issues i have with the game: 1. There is stability issues on the Quest 2 Version of this game,  2. It was difficult for me to find a server. 
and 3. There was very little told to you about the cheat mechanic. Granted, i believe i was playing on off hours where there are less players, and there is a mini tutorial behind a help menu. (Showing that tutorial automatically when you first enter a Death Poker lobby would alleviate this). Despite that, These issues were only a small bump in the road, as I feel the juice was worth the squeeze in this case. If you have a friend who owns a VR Headset, show them this game and i guarantee you will have a blast, even if its just a 1v1.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review.
We’re glad you enjoyed the twist we added to poker and slots, including wagering body parts and the new suit mechanic.

We also appreciate your feedback regarding Quest 2 stability, difficulty finding servers, and the lack of explanation around the cheating mechanic.
In particular, we agree that introducing the cheat tutorial more clearly when first entering a Death Poker lobby would help, and this is something we’ll be looking to improve in future updates.

For updates and to help players connect more easily, feel free to join our official Discord.
Thank you for playing Death Game Hotel.
